source_tree: clean public origin/main worktree plus uncommitted bench-q8-blocks --swap-rank2-shape measurement patch
scope: Exact Llama 3 8B Instruct Q8_0 retained-block lazy hot-path microbenchmarks; measurement evidence only.

bench_patch.purpose: reinterpret rank-2 rows/cols without transposing payload bytes so measurement can match Camelid's guarded rectangular lazy Q8 runtime layout for tensors such as ffn_gate and output.weight
bench_patch.compatibility: the JSON keeps shape as the logical shape and adds storage_shape, logical_shape, and swap_rank2_shape

interpretation.ffn_projection_observation: Representative 8B FFN lazy Q8 all-row dot over logical [14336,4096] costs about 36.7 ms per projection in retained-block serial microbench mode while avoiding 224 MiB f32 materialization for a 59.5 MiB Q8 tensor.
interpretation.logits_observation: Swapped logical output.weight [128256,4096] costs about 328 ms for one all-row dot and avoids about 2.0 GiB f32 materialization for a 532.3 MiB Q8 tensor, but still dominates single-token logits work.
interpretation.known_gap: This bench uses retained Q8 blocks, not the production file-backed reader path; pair it with response-local timing/RSS evidence before making performance-portability claims.

claim_boundary: Lazy Q8 hot-path cost evidence only for exact Llama 3 8B Instruct Q8_0 tensors. No broad Llama-family, full-context, arbitrary-template, neighboring-size, other-quantization, or portability support claim.

ubuntu_followup_validation.worktree: clean temp clone of public origin/main at 723a665 plus the same measurement-only bench-q8-blocks --swap-rank2-shape patch
ubuntu_followup_validation.notes: Follow-up release-mode JSON timings were consistent with the public bundle. The first shell /usr/bin/time sample included release build overhead, so process elapsed/RSS from that sample is intentionally not used as performance evidence.
